criteria.criterion: TestReaderDuringSplit replaces its sleep-based overlap assumption with a deterministic synchronization barrier (e.g. a signal channel fired at ExecuteSplitAtomic's atomic rename point).
criteria.check: Installed atomicCommitHook (existing execute.go seam) to close renamedCh at stage 'before_commit_append', which fires strictly after the redirect-stub content rename (writeNewContentFile) and strictly before WAL commit. Reader's fixed 400-iteration sleep bound removed in favor of stopCh-terminated loop.

criteria.criterion: The 'reader observed post-split content at least once' assertion no longer produces occasional false failures when the split completes before the reader's loop overlaps it.
criteria.check: One cs.Read(fileID) call issued directly after <-renamedCh, sequenced via channel happens-before to occur strictly after the rename; folded into the same reads slice validated by sawPostSplit check. Guarantees sawPostSplit >= 1 unconditionally, independent of goroutine scheduling.

criteria.criterion: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestReaderDuringSplit -count=50: 50/50 clean runs with no false failures.
criteria.check: Ran -count=50 (PASS, 50/50, 20.5s) and additionally -count=100 (PASS, 100/100, 27.3s) for extra confidence.

criteria.criterion: No behavior change to production code / package (invariant from scope constraint).
criteria.check: git status --short confirms only engine/split/split_race_test.go modified by this agent; atomicCommitHook was pre-existing production seam (already nil-in-production, already used by execute_test.go), not newly added.
go test ./engine/split/... -race: PASS (109.9s), no regressions in guard/orchestrate/execute/proposer tests despite those files being concurrently modified by other agents.
